subject: Following up on Wednesday’s NYU Alert Messages
body:
.

NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem



DATE:

Thursday, April 16, 2026

TO:

The NYU Community

FROM:

NYU Department of Campus Safety

RE:

Following up on Wednesday's NYU Alert Messages



Yesterday at 6:00 p.m., we tested the NYU Alert system, our notification system in the event of emergencies.

If you did not receive a text message from the University saying "NYU Alert - This is a test of NYU's Emergency Notification System…," it likely means that NYU doesn't have your correct mobile phone number. NYU community members in Shanghai should not expect to receive a test text message.

While we respect your privacy and do not wish to increase the amount of spam text messages you receive, it's vital that we have your correct mobile number.

Apart from two tests per year, we only send NYU Alert messages when there's an emergency or there's vital information you need, such as canceling in-person classes due to inclement weather. And we obviously would never share your numbers with vendors or anyone else for that matter.

The goal of NYU Alerts is to keep you safe. So, if you didn't receive yesterday's text message,
go to this page (https://t.e2ma.net/click/vfdvgq/bct513xb/f7vjsjc)
and make sure your mobile number is properly entered. If you believe you are already signed up but you still didn't receive the text, please email emergencymanagement@nyu.edu so that we can diagnose the issue and make sure you receive future notifications.

Thank you for your participation in keeping our community safe.



Campus Safety (https://t.e2ma.net/click/vfdvgq/bct513xb/vzwjsjc)
 Transportation (https://t.e2ma.net/click/vfdvgq/bct513xb/bsxjsjc)
 Frequently Asked Questions (https://t.e2ma.net/click/vfdvgq/bct513xb/rkyjsjc)


 (https://t.e2ma.net/click/vfdvgq/bct513xb/7czjsjc)

subject: Updates to our partner ads setting control
body:
Google Ads

Updates to our partner ads setting control

Dear Google User,

At Google, we believe you should always be in control of your data. That's  
why we've built My Ad Center and many Google Account tools, like Privacy  
Checkup, that make it easy to manage your data, privacy, and security  
settings.

Building on these controls, we’re introducing a new setting through Google  
Partner ad settings  
<https://c.gle/ADMV5J__Z1URlB860LHKbtWUOtxT0w2Ie0BlAKrZkdBUsbrnUz6dqvmPM4jH30K02a5Jyj-zJt6rJQ83PeuxFlKMzEeh2Wus1QfYCg5Z5smpANcyVzTDsQZYE5i4M51H-EYYg-HQL7LkbRL220kk-bxo6Y6HC8ieUL_u0sA>  
that lets you choose whether to provide additional information to  
advertising partners when you visit websites and apps that partner with  
Google. This data allows advertising partners to select which ads to show  
you and measure ad performance.

You can learn more about the tools you have available through Google  
<https://c.gle/ADMV5J_HQi4rGwdB3GYx9lozems6NSH2RVTErNJwDjkQB83TuTao0P0yDn6xkQroJwTBGR21GnhSgicpP_ruSRtBib53Wdteowu9o7vezingQLjTG-9iIYVR3sKY2TfQ2UOsegCHh6xwo70YRv2AmcbHcDJdFvpUbzQoscUCtctfk6SroODv1ZFesAO3qlY_psdZ>,  
and about controls across partner sites and apps  
<https://c.gle/ADMV5J8Q_G3Wf1JVcItaGJsyUo4zabPwfqdSPLAVNoXmqy03ffJoE-s-nBGI3uZVxJsc19yVMC2dy4sg872CN3bnbP0JvY-Y4xbGonJ0f-WD7e-FXcHsD-JuK-qIHraBgfXxN6aw9EEYWmssnkH5oBzd8hp1zKjSwLVUpM9kwcRvc2ZwFbCRdUATe8lQ9ntLEDjWdol9OQo4n6T0dwKiS7EYvSKE>.

Sincerely,
Your Google team

You've received this mandatory email service announcement to update you  
about important changes to your Google Ads account.

Google

subject: Fwd: Clery Campus Notice - Attempted Robbery
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Fountain L. Walker, Vice President, Global Campus Safety <
clery-notice@nyu.edu>
Date: Sat, Feb 21, 2026 at 5:01 AM
Subject: Clery Campus Notice - Attempted Robbery
To: <cl6750@nyu.edu>


.

.
[image: NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem]
------------------------------
NYU Clery Campus Notice
------------------------------
*Attempted Robbery*

On Saturday, February 21 at approximately 1:45 a.m., the Department of
Campus Safety received a report of an attempted robbery that occurred at
approximately 1:40 a.m., on West 3rd Street, outside 2 Washington Square
Village in Manhattan.

The victim, an NYU student, reported that they were walking east on West
3rd Street toward Mercer Street, when they were approached by six
individuals in ski masks. One individual told the student to hand over
their wallet, while two individuals in the group displayed box cutters. The
student ran into Shimkin Hall and reported the incident to a Campus Safety
Officer.

No injuries or lost property were reported by the student.

NYPD was notified and is investigating the incident.

*Description of Suspect(s)*: Video review showed the following:

- one suspect wearing a white, hooded sweatshirt with grey pants

- one suspect with a red, hooded sweatshirt, black vest, and black pants

- one suspect with a blue coat and grey pants

- one suspect with a black coat over a light colored, hooded sweatshirt and
pants

- two suspects in all black clothing

The NYU Department of Campus Safety will continue to monitor the incident
and provide updates as new information is received. If you have any
additional information about this incident, please contact
NYU Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
*Please Remember These Safety Tips:*


   - Be aware of your surroundings, especially when walking alone.
   - Using a smartphone or other electronic device, especially with ear
   buds, can be distracting and may make you a vulnerable target for an
   assailant.
   - Trust your instincts. If you feel that you may be in danger, seek out
   others, a store, or an open NYU facility that has Campus Safety Officers
   posted.
   - Try not to walk alone during late-night hours. Walk in groups,
   whenever you can, there is always safety in numbers.
   - If you are the victim of - or witness to - a crime, call 911
   immediately and then report the incident to the Department
   of Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
   - NYU Students can call the Wellness Exchange at (212) 443-9999, 24/7,
   for confidential medical and counseling support.
   - NYU Faculty, Administrators, and Staff can call the Employee
   Assistance Program powered by Optum at (888) 980-8740, 24/7, for
   confidential counseling support.

Check out our website to learn more about how to stay safe on campus and
around the city, including tips
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/1tvsxfb>,
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/hmwsxfb>
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/xexsxfb>
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/d7xsxfb>
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/tzysxfb>community engagement
events <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/9rzsxfb>, and digital
programming <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/pk0sxfb>.

Read more about our notification policy and procedures
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/5c1sxfb>. You can also learn more
about Campus Safety's services and review answers to Frequently Asked
Questions <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/l51sxfb> on our website.

Campus Safety <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/1x2sxfb>
Transportation <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/hq3sxfb>  Frequently
Asked Questions <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/xi4sxfb>
[image: Facebook] <https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/db5sxfb> [image:

<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/9v6sxfb> [image: TikTok]
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/lxbvfk/9ngrw8cc/po7sxfb>

subject: Fwd: Clery Campus Notice - Aggravated Assault
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Fountain L. Walker, Vice President, Global Campus Safety <
clery-notice@nyu.edu>
Date: Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 2:22 PM
Subject: Clery Campus Notice - Aggravated Assault
To: <cl6750@nyu.edu>


.

.
[image: NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem]
------------------------------
NYU Clery Campus Notice
------------------------------
Aggravated Assault

On Thursday, June 18, at 8:17 a.m., the Department of Campus Safety
received a report of a stabbing that occurred at approximately 8:05 p.m. on
Wednesday, June 17, in Washington Square Park in Manhattan.

The non-NYU victim confronted an individual in Washington Square Park.
During the altercation, several bystanders intervened. In response, a
family member of the victim deployed bear spray affecting those involved
and additional individuals in the surrounding area. A physical fight ensued
involving the victim, family member, and multiple other individuals. During
the fight, the victim was stabbed once in the chest with a knife. The
victim and family member then fled northbound toward University Place,
pursued by the persons of interest, whom the victim said were armed with
knives.

The victim and their family member were arrested by the NYPD for the use of
bear spray.

The stabbing is under investigation by the NYPD.

*Description of Suspect(s)*: The below photograph shows one of the two
individuals who was involved in the fight when the stabbing occurred and
this individual continued to pursue the victim through Washington Square
Park and onto University Place. The other individual involved remained in
the Park.

The NYU Department of Campus Safety will continue to monitor the incident.
If you have any additional information about this incident, please contact
the NYPD at 800-577-TIPS and NYU Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
*Please Remember These Safety* Tips:


   - Be aware of your surroundings, especially when walking alone.
   - Using a smartphone or other electronic device, especially with ear
   buds, can be distracting and may make you a vulnerable target for an
   assailant.
   - Trust your instincts. If you feel that you may be in danger, seek out
   others, a store, or an open NYU facility that has Campus Safety Officers
   posted.
   - Try not to walk alone during late-night hours. Walk in groups,
   whenever you can, there is always safety in numbers.
   - If you are the victim of - or witness to - a crime, call 911
   immediately and then report the incident to the Department
   of Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
   - NYU Students can call the Wellness Exchange at (212) 443-9999, 24/7,
   for confidential medical and counseling support.
   - NYU Faculty, Administrators, and Staff can call the Employee
   Assistance Program powered by Optum at (888) 980-8740, 24/7, for
   confidential counseling support.

Check out our website to learn more about how to stay safe on campus and
around the city, including tips
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/tbg6ok/9ngrw8cc/9ngbwgb>,

subject: Fwd: Clery Campus Notice - Attempted Robbery
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Fountain L. Walker, Vice President, Global Campus Safety <
clery-notice@nyu.edu>
Date: Mon, May 5, 2025 at 10:46 PM
Subject: Clery Campus Notice - Attempted Robbery
To: <cl6750@nyu.edu>


.

.
[image: NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem]
------------------------------
NYU Clery Campus Notice
------------------------------
Attempted Robbery

On Monday, May 5th, 2025, the Department of Campus Safety received a report
of an attempted robbery that occurred at approximately 8:17 p.m., at
Carlyle Court Residence Hall, in Manhattan.

The victim, A non-NYU community member, reported while walking a dog near
Carlyle Court Residence Hall, an unidentified individual threw an unknown
liquid at them. The victim entered the vestibule of Carlyle Court Residence
Hall, where the individual followed, grabbed the victim from behind, and
forced them to the ground in an attempt to steal their phone. The victim’s
dog bit the suspect, prompting the suspect to flee the scene.

The Non-NYU community member reported nothing was taken, and no physical
injuries. The NYPD was contacted and a report was taken.

*Description of Suspect(s)*: The suspect is masculine-presenting
approximately 5'9 - 5'10 with dark complexion wearing white sneakers with
no laces. No other details are available at this time.

The NYU Department of Campus Safety will continue to monitor the incident
and provide updates as new information is received. If you have any
additional information about this incident, please contact
NYU Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
*Please Remember These Safety Tips:*


   - Be aware of your surroundings, especially when walking alone.
   - Using a smartphone or other electronic device, especially with ear
   buds, can be distracting and may make you a vulnerable target for an
   assailant.
   - Trust your instincts. If you feel that you may be in danger, seek out
   others, a store, or an open NYU facility that has Campus Safety Officers
   posted.
   - Try not to walk alone during late-night hours. Walk in groups,
   whenever you can, there is always safety in numbers.
   - If you are the victim of - or witness to - a crime, call 911
   immediately and then report the incident to the Department
   of Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
   - NYU Students can call the Wellness Exchange at (212) 443-9999, 24/7,
   for confidential medical and counseling support.
   - NYU Faculty, Administrators, and Staff can call the Employee
   Assistance Program powered by Optum at (888) 980-8740, 24/7, for
   confidential counseling support.

Check out our website to learn more about how to stay safe on campus and
around the city, including tips

subject: Fwd: Clery Campus Notice - Motor Vehicle Thefts
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Fountain L. Walker, Vice President, Global Campus Safety <
clery-notice@nyu.edu>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 4:35 PM
Subject: Clery Campus Notice - Motor Vehicle Thefts
To: <cl6750@nyu.edu>


.

.
[image: NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem]
------------------------------
NYU Clery Campus Notice
------------------------------
Motor Vehicle Thefts

The NYU Department of Campus Safety recently received three reports of
Motor Vehicle Thefts involving an electric bike and electric scooters that
occurred within the last five days. These incidents occurred at and around
Schwartz Hall, located at 345 East 24th Street, in Manhattan.

The first complainant, an NYU student, reported that they locked their
e-scooter at the bike rack at Schwartz Hall on Thursday, July 3rd, at 11:00
a.m.. When they returned at 4:30 p.m., they realized the e-scooter was
stolen. The complainant filed a police report with the NYPD.

On July 8th, 2024, at 4:14 p.m., two suspects were observed taking an
e-bike locked up to a NYC bike rack located on the sidewalk in front of 433
1st Avenue. The suspects then moved to the bike rack at 338 East 25th
street, where they cut the lock and took an e-scooter belonging to an NYU
student. A police report was filed with the NYPD.

Below are photos of the individuals who are believed to have carried out
the thefts on July 8th. It is unknown if the suspects in the photo are
linked to the e-scooter theft on July 3rd.

If you have any additional information about this incident, please contact
the NYPD at 1(800) 577-TIPS and NYU Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
*Please Remember These Safety Tips:*


   - In addition to parking in a gated or designated parking area, consider
   using a heavy duty lock and/or chain to secure your bicycle or scooter.
   - Do not leave your bicycle or scooter for extended periods of time,
   such as overnight.
   - Consider registering your bicycles and other valuable property with
   Campus Safety's Violet Tag
   <https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/dzfltdb> program, which
   provides community members with the opportunity to place an adhesive
   sticker on valuable property, so that if it is lost or stolen and
   recovered, it can be returned to them. To register, please email
   dcs-community-engagement@nyu.edu
   <https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/trgltdb>.
   - If you witness behavior that seems odd or out of place, trust your
   instincts and report it to your nearest Campus Safety Officer.
   - Keep an inventory and photographs of valuable items for reference in
   case of loss or theft.
   - If you are the victim of - or witness to - a crime, call 911
   immediately and then report the incident to the Department
   of Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
   - NYU Students can call the Wellness Exchange at (212) 443-9999, 24/7,
   for confidential medical and counseling support.
   - NYU Faculty, Administrators, and Staff can call the Employee
   Assistance Program powered by Optum at (888) 980-8740, 24/7, for
   confidential counseling support.

Check out our website to learn more about how to stay safe on campus and
around the city, including tips
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/9jhltdb>,
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/pciltdb>

programming <https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/xamltdb>.

Read more about our notification policy and procedures
<https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/d3mltdb>. You can also learn more
about Campus Safety's services and review answers to Frequently Asked
Questions <https://t.e2ma.net/click/p4bzxj/9ngrw8cc/tvnltdb> on our website.

subject: Fwd: Clery Campus Notice - Robbery
body:
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Fountain L. Walker, Vice President, Global Campus Safety <
clery-notice@nyu.edu>
Date: Wed, Apr 1, 2026 at 8:51 AM
Subject: Clery Campus Notice - Robbery
To: <cl6750@nyu.edu>


.

.
[image: NEW YORK UNIVERSITY DEPARTMENT OF CAMPUS SAFETY Emblem]
------------------------------
NYU Clery Campus Notice
------------------------------
*Robbery*

On Wednesday, April 1, at approximately 5:00 a.m., the Department of Campus
Safety received a report of a robbery that occurred at approximately 4:50
a.m., on West 4th Street, outside Warren Weaver Hall in Manhattan.

The victim, a food vendor, reported that they were setting up on the corner
of West 4th Street and Mercer Street when they were approached by the four
suspects. One suspect said, "Give me my money, where is my money?" and
shoved the victim, while another attempted to punch the victim. The victim
fled west, and then the suspects took the victim's phone from their cart
and fled east toward Broadway.

No injuries were reported by the victim.

NYPD was notified and is investigating the incident.

*Description of Suspect(s)*: The suspects were described as three masculine
presenting and one feminine presenting individuals with dark complexions.
They appeared to be in their mid 20's. Photos are provided below.

The NYU Department of Campus Safety will continue to monitor the incident
and provide updates as new information is received. If you have any
additional information about this incident, please contact
NYU Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
*Please Remember These Safety Tips:*


   - Be aware of your surroundings, especially when walking alone.
   - Using a smartphone or other electronic device, especially with ear
   buds, can be distracting and may make you a vulnerable target for an
   assailant.
   - Trust your instincts. If you feel that you may be in danger, seek out
   others, a store, or an open NYU facility that has Campus Safety Officers
   posted.
   - Try not to walk alone during late-night hours. Walk in groups,
   whenever you can, there is always safety in numbers.
   - If you are the victim of - or witness to - a crime, call 911
   immediately and then report the incident to the Department
   of Campus Safety at (212) 998-2222.
   - NYU Students can call the Wellness Exchange at (212) 443-9999, 24/7,
   for confidential medical and counseling support.

subject: New privacy settings for Search services and Google Play
body:
Google Logo
 

lmgnewsproject@gmail.com
 

Hello LiveMediaGroup,

We’re updating our settings to give you even more control over saved  
history and personalized recommendations across Google Search services and  
Google Play. Search services include Search, Maps, Shopping, Hotels,  
Flights, Translate, and News. You’ll see this change in your Google Account  
in the next few days.

What’s changing

Previously, saving history and personalization were managed by Web & App  
Activity. Going forward, you can better tailor your Search services  
experience using your new Search Services History and Personalized  
Recommendations settings. These settings let you revisit your past searches  
and decide if you want your experience to be personalized. As people  
increasingly search in new ways, like searching a photo with Lens, Search  
Services History now includes media from your interactions, which you can  
stop saving at any time. For Google Play, you'll have new Play History and  
Personalization in Play settings, even if you’ve never used this service.  
You can turn these settings on or off at any time.

How this affects your account

New history and personalization settings: Your new settings for Search  
services and Google Play will reflect your most recent choices for Web &  
App Activity and Search Personalization settings. If Web & App Activity is  
currently off, these new settings will stay off. After the transition, the  
Web & App Activity setting will be separate from Search services’ and  
Play’s personalization and history settings. Changes to one setting won’t  
affect the others.

Auto-delete: Your prior choice from Web & App Activity for how long your  
history is saved will also apply to Search Services History and Play  
History. As always, you can change how long history is saved, choose an  
auto-delete period, and review or delete history manually at any time.

Your media is saved when Search Services History is on: Saved media  
includes your images, files, audio and video from your interactions with  
Search services to help improve your experience. For example, this lets you  
revisit your past visual searches with Lens or continue a Search Live  
conversation about a song you heard. To support these types of interactive  
product experiences, Google will now save your media to your Search  
Services History, applying robust privacy and security protections. Like  
your Search Services History, your saved media is also used to develop and  
improve Google services and technologies, including AI models and safety  
measures. With this transition, if your Web & App Activity setting is  
currently turned on, this new Save Media subsetting will also be turned on  
along with your Search Services History. You can turn off your  
<https://c.gle/AOPyDKSyyIElCg8ypohzXrHF1pRQhsh56yn_X-LhoTgjD6dyQ3yVU2c7KTt4-b_aVgbbtlLhU-C2YmVMa5wr0kUqJ6EVmS2afckMlpA1yZv9jh0tzsJJw2msAZ3vZuMdMqufyMJdWqdietSbF4W_Al_SOMS-oEcg9MJOpZVeuDUgBLDzBaD31vLLE3XHCnaxSnWQy7ndeiCSkxClxdl8s64KXC1Ljb_uwkcIaBAwv8bI5C34tMzxHLkX7IPYe9jkWSNOf5CpdpbpwvIFsrMSXDxbxYfBRuq77Fjso0O5BaBHFlYi9vPpIrA-EvYzj_4j_0qVYGkn44hHpfGPc7ef0KBJm6V373TWSrUujKVk_upqrSEE715qhAxRLTZNTxr0ZNAp2wwZ>Save  
Media subsetting at any time, and you can also delete individual pieces of  
media from your history.

You can manage these preferences by visiting
